title: Bounded Host Toolchain Materialization Authority Decision
objective: Decide whether and how a later bounded packet may materialize the minimum host toolchain required for Milestone 2 application and shared-package validation, without performing installs, package mutation, lockfile mutation, runtime mutation, service mutation, feature delivery, hosting transition, or old-clone mutation inside this decision packet.
active_role: Olares Developer Residency Toolchain Authority Operator
dependency_note: Packet 003 selected Branch B because Packet 002 showed missing host no-install toolchain surfaces: `pnpm` absent, root and operations-web `node_modules` absent, `pytest` absent, and `npx --no-install` canceled rather than downloading. Packet 004 may decide the authority shape for a later bounded materialization or defer path, but must not perform materialization itself.
required_inputs: Packet 002 host path and old-clone evidence
Packet 002 blocked validation attempts for `apps/operations-web` and `packages/calc-engine`
The Milestone 2 requirement for one active application lane and one shared package lane to validate from the Olares host
The still-closed no-go boundary around feature delivery, runtime/service mutation, public ingress, hosting transition, old-clone mutation, remote rewrite, rollback, force, reset, and clean
decision_requirements: Name the exact minimum host toolchain surfaces required before Milestone 2 validation can run.
Separate toolchain materialization authority from actual install or download execution.
Preserve GitHub as canonical and `/home/olares/code/apex` as the host mirror.
Keep package files and lockfiles unchanged unless a later packet explicitly scopes and validates any change.
Keep feature delivery closed until host-side validation readiness is proven by a later execution packet.
explicit_no_go_items: performing installs or downloads inside Packet 004
package or lockfile mutation inside Packet 004
sql_permission_note: Decision only. This packet authorizes no database writes, installs, package mutation, lockfile mutation, runtime mutation, service mutation, public ingress change, feature delivery, old-clone mutation, remote rewrite, rollback, force, reset, or clean.
decision_rationale: Packet 003 already proved the blocker surface is real rather than a PATH-discovery issue: `pnpm`, dependency trees, and `pytest` are absent under the current no-install boundary.

A truthful Milestone 2 path still exists, but only if toolchain materialization is separately packetized and bounded to the minimum host-local surfaces required for `apps/operations-web` and `packages/calc-engine` validation.
Deferring again would add no new evidence and would leave the project blocked on a known authority gap rather than a technical unknown.

Packet 004 can therefore authorize a later execution packet while still keeping installs, downloads, package mutation, lockfile mutation, runtime or service mutation, feature delivery, hosting transition, and old-clone mutation closed inside this decision packet.
minimum_toolchain_surfaces: A usable host-local `pnpm` path for `apps/operations-web` validation commands.
A materialized dependency tree sufficient for `apps/operations-web` TypeScript or build validation on the host.
Python test tooling sufficient to run the admitted `packages/calc-engine` validation command on the host.
No package.json or lockfile mutation unless a later execution packet proves it is required and explicitly scopes that change.
next_packet_candidate: Olares Dev Residency 005 - Minimum Host Toolchain Materialization Execution
closure_note: Executed 2026-05-05 as a decision-only authority gate. Packet 004 authorizes a later bounded minimum-scope host toolchain materialization execution packet, but performs no installs, downloads, dependency materialization, package or lockfile mutation, runtime or service mutation, feature delivery, hosting transition, old-clone mutation, remote rewrite, rollback, force, reset, or clean.
next_action: Execute `Olares Dev Residency 005 - Minimum Host Toolchain Materialization Execution` only as a later bounded packet and only on the minimum host-local toolchain surfaces required for Milestone 2 validation.
